name: Haemorrhagic Septicaemia
description: A highly fatal, acute septicaemic disease of cattle and buffaloes caused by Pasteurella multocida. Also known as Shipping Fever in temperate regions.

motion_magnitude.description: Very low – severe depression, reluctance to move

clinical_signs: Painful, hot, doughy swelling of throat, dewlap, and brisket
Severe respiratory distress – animal may breathe with mouth open
Death may occur within 6–24 hours of signs appearing
Petechial haemorrhages visible on mucous membranes
recommendations: Oxytetracycline or sulphonamides are treatment of choice
Vaccinate all animals annually with HS vaccine
Isolate sick animal and disinfect shared water sources
Notify local animal health authorities (notifiable disease)
Provide shade and reduce heat stress
Check for predisposing factors: overcrowding, malnutrition
references: Radostits et al., Veterinary Medicine 10th Ed.
